Data Analytics · Business Intelligence

Atharva
Bidwalkar

I am a data analyst with a Master's in Business Analytics from DePaul University, based in Mumbai. I work across the full analytics workflow: gathering and cleaning raw data, building structured data models in SQL, creating interactive dashboards in Power BI and Tableau, and communicating findings in a way that drives real decisions. I have experience with star schema design, DAX measures, medallion architecture, and interactive web-based data applications. Whether it is a 200,000-row flight dataset or a 568K-record live aviation feed, I focus on turning complexity into clarity. I am actively looking for data analyst and business intelligence roles where I can bring both technical depth and storytelling to the work.

Projects

↗
Interactive Web App

NYC Flight Routes Intelligence

An interactive 3D globe that maps every flight route out of JFK, EWR, and LGA, coloured by airline market share competition. Built on real BTS T-100 2025 data with monthly filtering, airline breakdown, and live competition analysis across contested, partial, and monopoly routes.

Netflix Content Analytics

An interactive Tableau dashboard exploring Netflix's global content library, covering movie vs TV show distribution, a choropleth map by country, top genres, rating breakdowns, and year-over-year growth from 2008 to 2020.

↗
SQL · Data Warehouse

SQL Exploratory Data Analysis

An end-to-end EDA project in MySQL, built across a Bronze, Silver, and Gold medallion architecture. CRM and ERP source data is ingested, cleaned, and modelled into dimension and fact tables, then explored through magnitude, date range, and ranking queries to surface actionable business insights.

MySQLMedallion Architecture CRM/ERP dataWindow functions Ranking analysis

04

Python: Weather Time Series

43,824observations
5 yrhistorical span
2.40 Cforecast MAE
Weather Analysis Pipeline
Open-Meteo API · scikit-learn · pandas · matplotlib
  • Fetches live and historical hourly weather data from the Open-Meteo API for any city via geocoding, stitching a five-year archive with a fresh 16-day forecast in a single unified dataset.
  • Runs time-series decomposition into trend, seasonal, and residual components, with an ADF stationarity test to validate modelling assumptions before forecasting.
  • Flags weather anomalies using an Isolation Forest model trained on temperature, humidity, and pressure features
  • Benchmarks a custom linear regression forecast head-to-head against Open-Meteo's professional forecast.
  • Historical data is disk-cached after the first run, making subsequent re-runs near-instant while always fetching a fresh forecast reflecting today's conditions.
Pythonpandasscikit-learn Isolation ForestTime-series decomposition Open-Meteo APImatplotlib
View Notebook on GitHub ↗

About

I am a data analyst with a background in economic research and intelligence. I hold a Master's in Business Analytics from DePaul University and am based in Mumbai, India.

My focus is building end-to-end analytics projects, from raw SQL pipelines and star schemas through to Power BI and Tableau dashboards that tell a clear story to any audience.

The NYC Flight Routes project reflects where I push myself: bridging serious data engineering with front-end craft that makes insights genuinely engaging to explore.
